body.title: Fix scope-backfill UPDATE firing every connection (38k hits in db_queries.log)
body.result: Superseded by D-sqlite-removal-2026-04-22. The 38k UPDATE hits were historical — Postgres gate (scope_backfill_123 in _migrations) already shipped 2026-04-17. Residual 3 hits/9 days came from the SQLite fallback path, which is being removed entirely instead of gated. See T-test-postgres-schema + T-remove-sqlite-fallback.
body.description:
UPDATE memories SET scope = ? WHERE portable = 0 AND scope = ? fires 38,034 times in db_queries.log (avg 68ms, worst 502ms). Same pattern as T-cortex-261 (meaning_to_me layer fix, now closed) — a migration that should be one-shot but runs on every connection because it lacks a _migrations guard. Add a 'scope_backfill' entry to _SCHEMA_MIGRATIONS / _migrations table so it runs exactly once.

**Affected files:** TBD discovery step — likely wild_igor/igor/memory/cortex.py migration list, possibly lab/utility_closet/db_proxy.py. Will confirm on sprint start. Update docstring on the migration runner that guards it.
**Design rules:** no-sqlite: touches _migrations table (Postgres existing) — no new tables, no SQLite. oop-first: migration guard is a single conditional check, no shared state. docs-in-code: update the migration runner file's docstring to name the new migration entry. test-plan-or-why-not: applies.
**Scope boundary:** IN — add _migrations guard so UPDATE runs once. OUT — restructuring _migrations schema, schema changes to memories table.
**Test plan:** Integration test on real Postgres — run the migration path twice, assert UPDATE appears 0 times in db_queries.log on second run. Pattern from T-cortex-261 tests applies directly.
